name: Aembit
description: Aembit provides non-human identity and access management, giving workloads secretless, policy-based, just-in-time access to sensitive resources instead of relying on long-lived API keys or embedded credentials.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces static secrets/API keys with dynamic workload identity
Extends the IAM paradigm from humans to machines and AI agents
attributes.problemStatement: Non-human identities such as service accounts, API keys, and AI agents now outnumber human identities in modern cloud environments but lack the lifecycle governance, just-in-time access, and secretless authentication that human IAM already provides, leaving credentials long-lived, over-privileged, and exposed.
attributes.productDescription: Aembit issues short-lived, policy-scoped credentials to workloads at request time based on verified workload identity and posture, replacing hardcoded secrets and static API keys across cloud, SaaS, and data-center environments.

founders.name: David Goldschlag
founders.background: Named as co-founder and CEO on Aembit's Series A press release; detailed prior operating history not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

founders.name: Kevin Sapp
founders.background: Named as co-founder on Aembit's Series A press release; detailed prior operating history not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

name: P0 Security
description: P0 Security provides an agentless, just-in-time cloud access control plane that replaces standing privileges with short-lived, auditable access for both human and non-human identities across AWS, GCP, and Azure.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces standing cloud privileges with ephemeral just-in-time access
Consolidates human and non-human identity governance in one control plane
attributes.problemStatement: Cloud engineers and non-human identities accumulate standing privileged access that is rarely revoked, creating a large, hard-to-audit attack surface across AWS, GCP, and Azure that PAM tools built for on-premises systems do not address.
attributes.productDescription: P0's AuthZ Control Plane grants short-lived, scoped, auditable access to servers, databases, cloud consoles, and Kubernetes clusters through Slack, CLI, or automation, revoking access automatically once a task is complete.

founders.name: Shashwat Sehgal
founders.background: Presented publicly as CEO at P0's RSAC Innovation Sandbox finalist appearance; detailed prior employer history not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

founders.name: Gergely Danyi
founders.background: Named on P0's own about page; detailed prior employer history not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

founders.name: Nathan Brahms
founders.background: Named on P0's own about page; detailed prior employer history not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

name: Nudge Security
description: Nudge Security is a SaaS and AI security governance platform that continuously discovers SaaS and AI application usage across an organization and delivers real-time behavioral guidance to employees to reduce shadow-IT and shadow-AI risk.

industryPath: Shadow IT and shadow AI security
targetCustomers: Mid-market and enterprise organizations with distributed SaaS/AI adoption
technicalApproaches: SaaS discovery via email/expense/network signal analysis
disruptionMechanisms: Replaces manual/periodic SaaS audits with continuous automated discovery
Uses positive behavioral nudges instead of hard-block controls
attributes.problemStatement: Employees adopt SaaS and AI tools faster than IT can track them, creating shadow-IT sprawl, ungoverned data flows, and identity risk that periodic manual audits cannot keep up with.
attributes.productDescription: Nudge Security discovers SaaS and AI applications in use, assesses risk and security posture, manages associated identities, and delivers real-time behavioral guidance through browser notifications and workplace chat tools.

founders.name: Russell Spitler
founders.background: Held product and strategy leadership roles at AlienVault and AT&T Cybersecurity; co-founded the Open Threat Exchange with Jaime Blasco.

founders.name: Jaime Blasco
founders.background: Led AT&T Alien Labs (formerly AlienVault Labs) as a security researcher; thirteen-year working history with Spitler predating Nudge Security.

name: Tobiko Data
description: Tobiko Data builds SQLMesh and SQLGlot, open-source data transformation and SQL-parsing tools positioned as a state-aware, cost-efficient alternative to dbt, plus Tobiko Cloud, an enterprise-grade managed platform on top.

targetCustomers: Mid-market to enterprise data platform teams
technicalApproaches: Open-source core (SQLMesh, SQLGlot) with managed cloud
founderArchetypes: Technical open-source maintainers from Big Tech (Airbnb, Netflix)
disruptionMechanisms: Reduces redundant recomputation and warehouse spend versus dbt-style full-refresh models
Open-source-led adoption competing with the entrenched dbt incumbency
attributes.businessModel: Open-core: free OSS (SQLMesh/SQLGlot) plus paid Tobiko Cloud enterprise platform
attributes.goToMarket: Developer-led / open-source-led with an enterprise sales overlay
attributes.problemStatement: Data teams using dbt-style full-refresh transformation frameworks pay for redundant compute and lose confidence in change correctness as pipelines scale, without built-in state-awareness, environment isolation, or automated impact analysis.
attributes.productDescription: SQLMesh tracks what has actually changed in a data pipeline and only reprocesses what is necessary, lowering warehouse compute costs versus full-refresh transformation tools; Tobiko Cloud adds collaboration and governance on top.

founders.name: Tyson Mao
founders.background: Named on Tobiko's own about page as co-founder and CEO; detailed prior operating background not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

founders.name: Toby Mao
founders.background: Previously senior staff engineer at Airbnb leading the XP experimentation and Minerva metrics platforms; earlier led the XP platform at Netflix.

founders.name: Iaroslav Zeigerman
founders.background: Previously a senior software engineer on Netflix's data team, where he worked with co-founder Toby Mao.

name: Turso
description: Turso is a Rust-rewritten, SQLite-compatible database built on the open-source libSQL engine that lets developers create millions of small, isolated, file-based databases for multi-tenant SaaS, AI agents, and edge workloads.

founderArchetypes: Systems/database infrastructure engineers (ex-ScyllaDB, ex-Datadog, former Linux kernel maintainer)
disruptionMechanisms: Replaces single shared-server database architecture with millions of lightweight per-tenant databases
Brings SQLite to distributed/edge and agentic workloads it was not originally designed for
attributes.businessModel: Usage-based self-serve subscription tiers (free, $4.99/mo Developer, Scaler, Pro) plus enterprise
attributes.problemStatement: Modern multi-tenant SaaS and AI-agent applications need thousands to millions of small, isolated databases with edge-local latency, a pattern traditional single-server SQL databases and most serverless databases are not architected to serve cheaply or simply.
attributes.productDescription: Turso treats each database as a lightweight file rather than a running server process, allowing instant creation, global replication, and per-tenant or per-agent isolation, with concurrent writes and native vector search built in.

founders.name: Glauber Costa
founders.background: Former VP of Field Engineering at ScyllaDB and former staff engineer at Datadog, per Turso's own investors page.

founders.name: Pekka Enberg
founders.background: Early employee at ScyllaDB and a former Linux kernel maintainer, per Turso's own investors page.

name: Estuary
description: Estuary builds Flow, a unified right-time data platform that merges change data capture, streaming, and batch data movement into a single low-latency runtime across more than 200 source and destination systems.

productCategoryPath: Unified batch and streaming data movement
targetCustomers: Enterprise IT in finance, healthcare, logistics, and SaaS
technicalApproaches: Unified batch/streaming runtime built on the Gazette broker
founderArchetypes: Infrastructure engineers/operators from Google, YouTube, and a prior acquisition exit (LiveRamp/Arbor)
disruptionMechanisms: Collapses separate batch-ELT and streaming-CDC tools into one platform
Sub-100ms latency versus traditional batch-only ELT tools
attributes.goToMarket: Enterprise sales with self-serve free trial
attributes.problemStatement: Enterprises run separate, duplicative tools for batch ELT and real-time streaming/CDC, creating cost, latency, and reliability gaps as AI and operational use cases demand fresher data than nightly batch jobs can deliver.
attributes.productDescription: Estuary Flow captures, transforms, and synchronizes data with sub-100ms latency and exactly-once delivery across 200-plus connectors, serving both batch ELT and real-time streaming use cases from one pipeline definition.

founders.name: David Yaffe
founders.background: Previously COO of LiveRamp and co-founder/CEO of Arbor, which LiveRamp acquired in 2016.

founders.name: Johnny Graettinger
founders.background: Prior infrastructure engineering experience at Google and YouTube.

name: RunReveal
description: RunReveal is a unified, AI-native security data platform built as a modern alternative to legacy SIEM, combining a security data lake, log pipelines, and AI-assisted detection and investigation.

productCategoryPath: AI-native security data lake and detection
targetCustomers: Platform/DevOps-adjacent security teams at technology companies
technicalApproaches: AI chat/MCP-based investigation with bring-your-own-LLM support
disruptionMechanisms: Replaces legacy per-gigabyte SIEM pricing with a modern data-stack cost structure
AI-assisted investigation reduces manual log-analysis time
attributes.problemStatement: Legacy SIEM platforms charge premium per-gigabyte prices for log ingestion and still leave security teams manually correlating alerts, forcing a tradeoff between visibility and cost that AI-native, modern-data-stack architectures can remove.
attributes.productDescription: RunReveal ingests, routes, and stores terabytes of security data cost-efficiently, layering detection-as-code and conversational AI investigation (via chat and MCP) on top to replace legacy SIEM workflows.

founders.name: Evan Johnson
founders.background: Named as a co-founder on RunReveal's own funding announcement; specific prior employer not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

founders.name: Alan Braithwaite
founders.background: Named as a co-founder on RunReveal's own funding announcement; specific prior employer not confirmed in public sources reviewed.

name: Twine Security
description: Twine Security builds AI 'digital employees' that perform recurring cybersecurity operations tasks, starting with identity and access management, autonomously detecting, analyzing, planning, and remediating identity risk.

targetCustomers: Fortune 500 healthcare and food and beverage organizations (disclosed reference sectors)
founderArchetypes: Repeat cybersecurity operators (former Claroty leadership team)
disruptionMechanisms: Substitutes AI 'digital employee' labor for manual identity-governance analyst work such as access reviews and entitlement cleanup
attributes.problemStatement: Identity and access management teams face a chronic shortage of skilled analysts to perform recurring, high-volume tasks like user access reviews, stale-account cleanup, and entitlement optimization, leaving organizations with access sprawl that manual review cannot keep pace with.
attributes.productDescription: Twine's AI digital employee, Alex, detects identity and access issues, analyzes the underlying risk, plans a remediation approach, and executes remediation across tasks including access reviews, stale-account cleanup, and least-privilege enforcement.

founders.name: Benny Porat
founders.background: Reported by CTech (Calcalist) as a former senior manager at Claroty prior to co-founding Twine in 2024; specific title at Twine not confirmed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Omri Green
founders.background: Reported by CTech (Calcalist) as a former senior manager at Claroty prior to co-founding Twine in 2024; specific title at Twine not confirmed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Justin Woody
founders.background: Reported by CTech (Calcalist) as a former senior manager at Claroty prior to co-founding Twine in 2024; specific title at Twine not confirmed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Nadav Erez
founders.background: Reported by CTech (Calcalist) as a former senior manager at Claroty prior to co-founding Twine in 2024; specific title at Twine not confirmed in sources reviewed.

name: Edera
description: Edera builds a hardened runtime that brings virtual-machine-grade isolation to containers using a Rust-rewritten Type 1 hypervisor, extending the approach to secure AI/GPU workloads through automated GPU configuration and isolation.

productCategoryPath: Hypervisor-grade container and AI/GPU workload isolation
technicalApproaches: Type-1 hypervisor isolation rewritten in Rust
No shared kernel state between containers
Automated GPU configuration/isolation for AI workloads
founderArchetypes: Security infrastructure operator (ex-Chainguard) and systems engineer
disruptionMechanisms: Replaces shared-kernel Linux namespace container isolation with VM-grade per-container isolation without traditional VM performance/ops overhead
attributes.problemStatement: Multi-tenant Kubernetes and AI/GPU workloads share a kernel with weak isolation guarantees, so a single container escape or misconfigured GPU driver can compromise an entire cluster, a risk traditional namespace-based isolation and manual hardening do not fully close.
attributes.productDescription: Edera Protect runs each container as an isolated hypervisor guest with no shared kernel state, and Edera Protect AI automates GPU detection, configuration, and isolation to secure AI infrastructure against container-escape and driver vulnerabilities.

founders.name: Emily Long
founders.background: Previously at Chainguard prior to co-founding Edera, per company financing materials.

founders.name: Alex Zenla
founders.background: Described in company and press materials as an experienced systems and cybersecurity engineer; specific prior employers not detailed in sources reviewed.

name: Prophet Security
description: Prophet Security's Agentic AI SOC Platform autonomously triages, investigates, and responds to security alerts, proactively hunts for threats, and tunes detections, aiming to automate the manual work that dominates SOC analyst time.

productCategoryPath: Autonomous SOC alert triage and investigation
founderArchetypes: Repeat cybersecurity entrepreneurs (StackRox founding team, acquired by Red Hat)
disruptionMechanisms: Automates L1/L2 SOC analyst alert triage that today consumes most analyst time
Reduces false-positive load through AI-driven detection tuning
attributes.problemStatement: SOC teams are overwhelmed by alert volume and spend most of their time on manual triage and investigation rather than genuine threats, a workload gap that has outpaced hiring and that rule-based SOAR automation has not fully solved.
attributes.productDescription: Prophet's platform includes an AI SOC Analyst that autonomously triages and investigates alerts, an AI Threat Hunter that proactively investigates across environments, and an AI Detection Advisor that analyzes telemetry to close detection gaps aligned to MITRE ATT&CK.

founders.name: Kamal Shah
founders.background: Previously CEO of StackRox, a Kubernetes-native security company acquired by Red Hat.

founders.name: Vibhav Sreekanti
founders.background: Previously VP of Engineering at StackRox, then Senior Director of Software Engineering at Red Hat; earlier co-founder/CTO of Defend7 and Senior Director of Software Development at Oracle.
